meta.title: BrowsePad – TV-First Web Browser for Android TV
meta.description: BrowsePad redefines web browsing on Android TV with BrowsePad Connect, a phone-powered trackpad experience that makes TV browsing feel faster, more natural, and more precise.
meta.keywords: BrowsePad, Android TV browser, TV web browser, browser for Android TV, Google TV browser, Fire TV browser, BrowsePad Connect, Android TV trackpad browser, phone trackpad for TV browser, mobile remote browser, Android TV pointer browser, TV browsing redefined, Android TV browsing experience, Leanback browser, phone remote browser
meta.author: Deepak PK

og.title: BrowsePad for Android TV
og.description: BrowsePad redefines how people browse the web on Android TV with a phone-based trackpad remote, precise pointer control, and a TV-first browser experience.

twitter.title: BrowsePad for Android TV
twitter.description: A TV browser built around phone-based trackpad control, making Android TV web browsing feel more natural and precise.

product.name: BrowsePad
product.heroTitle: BrowsePad – Redefining Web Browsing on Android TV
product.heroDescription: BrowsePad is built around BrowsePad Connect, a phone-powered trackpad experience that makes browsing on Android TV, Google TV, and Fire TV feel faster, more natural, and more precise than a traditional TV browser.

stats.label: A New Way to Browse on TV
Natural Web Control on Big Screens

cards.title: Built Around a Better Browsing Method
bullets: BrowsePad is designed around phone-powered trackpad input, not just basic D-pad navigation
It brings a more natural browsing flow to Android TV, Google TV, and Fire TV
Smooth handoff between toolbar focus mode and pointer mode keeps navigation fluid
Browser chrome can auto-hide while browsing and return when needed

cards.title: Tabs, Bookmarks, and History
bullets: Create, switch, close, and close all tabs from a TV-friendly tab strip
Per-tab titles and favicons make open pages easy to scan
Save bookmarks from the toolbar and review them in a dedicated Bookmarks page
Automatic browsing history with clear-history confirmation and session restore

cards.title: Smart Address Bar and Search
bullets: Address bar accepts full URLs, bare domains, and search queries
Automatic search-vs-URL resolution keeps navigation simple on TV
Choose your default search engine from Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Brave Search, Perplexity, or Andi
Built-in `browsepad://connect` route opens the BrowsePad Connect experience instantly

cards.title: Precision Pointer Control
bullets: Visible app-wide pointer works across browser chrome, internal pages, and website content
Phone trackpad input gives users finer control than a typical TV browser remote flow
Use the D-pad for precise nudging when needed, or rely on the phone for fluid movement
Pointer edge scrolling helps reach content inside long web pages

cards.title: BrowsePad Connect Changes the Experience
bullets: Built-in Connect page is available as a native TV tab
QR code pairing makes it fast to turn a phone into a browsing trackpad
Controller URL is shown on the TV for manual pairing when needed
Connection status, disconnect summaries, and port fallback improve reliability

cards.title: Phone Input That Feels Like Real Browsing
bullets: Phone controller supports single-finger cursor movement and tap-to-click
Use two-finger gestures for right-click and scrolling, plus fling momentum
Pinch-to-zoom and phone-side pointer speed settings make complex sites easier to handle
Remote text input works for the address bar, webpage fields, textareas, and content-editable areas

cards.title: View Modes and Settings
bullets: Switch each site between Adaptive and Desktop view modes
Set a default view mode for sites without a saved override
Settings include search engine choice, cache clear, and app version display
Support pages like Help, Privacy Policy, Terms, About, and feedback open inside the browser

cards.title: Downloads and Web Handling
bullets: Download interception asks for confirmation before starting a file download
Android DownloadManager integration provides start and completion feedback
Context menu actions include open in new tab, copy link, and download resource
Friendly internal error pages cover common failures like timeouts, SSL issues, and connection errors

cards.title: Store and Subscription Support
bullets: Separate builds support Google Play, and Huawei AppGallery
Store-specific subscription flows are integrated for each distribution channel
Subscription management entry points are available from the app menu
Recent subscription verification is cached with an offline grace period

items.title: Users Who Hate D-pad Browsing
items.text: BrowsePad is for people who want web browsing on TV to feel less like fighting a remote and more like using a real pointing device.

items.title: Precision-Heavy Websites
items.text: Sites with small controls, hover states, long pages, and text fields become easier to use when your phone acts as the trackpad.

items.title: A New TV Browsing Habit
items.text: BrowsePad encourages a different browsing behavior on TV by combining the big screen with the familiarity of phone gestures and touch-like precision.

items.title: More Than Another TV Browser
items.text: The goal is not just to match other Android TV browsers, but to redefine how browsing should feel on TV through BrowsePad Connect.

items.a: BrowsePad is an Android TV browser built around BrowsePad Connect, a phone-powered trackpad system designed to redefine how people browse the web on TV.

items.q: How do I control BrowsePad on a TV?
items.a: You can use a regular TV remote, but BrowsePad is designed to shine when paired with a phone through BrowsePad Connect for trackpad-style control, scrolling, zooming, and text input.
Yes. BrowsePad supports creating, switching, closing, and closing all tabs, with per-tab titles and favicons shown in the tab strip.

items.q: Can I use my phone as a remote?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad Connect serves a mobile controller from the TV app over your local network. The phone controller supports cursor movement, click, right-click, scrolling, pinch-to-zoom, and remote text entry so browsing feels more natural on a TV.

items.q: What search engines can I choose from?
items.a: BrowsePad includes Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Brave Search, Perplexity, and Andi as built-in default search engine options.

items.q: Can websites open in desktop mode?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad supports per-site view mode switching between Adaptive and Desktop, helping the phone-trackpad experience work better across a wider range of websites.

items.q: Does BrowsePad save bookmarks and history?
items.a: Yes. You can save bookmarks from the toolbar, review them in a dedicated Bookmarks page, and revisit previously opened pages from History. Session restore also brings back previously open non-internal tabs after relaunch.

items.q: Can I download files from websites?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad intercepts downloads, asks for confirmation before starting them, and integrates with Android DownloadManager where supported.

items.q: What happens when a page fails to load?
items.a: BrowsePad shows a friendly internal error page and handles common failures such as host lookup problems, connection failures, timeouts, SSL failures, redirect loops, I/O issues, and unsupported schemes.

howItWorks.title: Start a Better Way to Browse on TV

steps.title: Open BrowsePad
steps.text: Launch BrowsePad on your Android TV, Google TV, or Fire TV device and open a browser experience designed to work with a phone-powered trackpad.

steps.title: Enter a URL or Search
steps.text: Use the address bar for direct URLs, bare domains, or search queries while BrowsePad handles the web basics with a TV-friendly interface.

steps.title: Pair a Phone and Feel the Difference
steps.text: Open BrowsePad Connect, scan the QR code, and turn your phone into the input method that makes browsing on TV feel fluid, precise, and natural.

cards.title: Real Browser Basics
bullets: Open, switch, and close tabs from a tab strip built for TV viewing
Favicons and titles keep multiple open pages easy to manage

cards.title: Trackpad-Like Precision
bullets: Visible pointer overlay works across both browser UI and webpage content
Phone-driven pointer control makes hard-to-target sites much easier to use

cards.title: Phone as the Main Input
bullets: Connect a phone quickly with QR pairing over your local network
Use gesture-based cursor control, scrolling, zooming, and remote text input

cards.title: Desktop Sites Work Better
bullets: Switch per-site view mode between Adaptive and Desktop
Save bookmarks, review history, and clear cached web data from settings

items.title: People Frustrated by TV Browser Remotes
items.text: BrowsePad is for users who want TV browsing to feel less clumsy and more like natural pointer-driven web use.

items.title: Users Who Already Reach for Their Phone
items.text: If the phone is already in your hand, BrowsePad turns it into the control surface that makes TV browsing finally feel right.

items.title: Websites That Need Precision
items.text: Search-heavy pages, dense menus, hover interactions, long forms, and smaller UI controls benefit from trackpad-style input.

items.title: Anyone Who Wants a Better TV Browsing Model
items.text: BrowsePad is not just about loading websites on TV. It is about changing how browsing should work on TV in the first place.

items.a: BrowsePad is an Android TV browser built to redefine how browsing works on TV by centering the experience around BrowsePad Connect and phone-powered trackpad control.

items.q: How do I browse if a normal TV remote feels limited?
items.a: BrowsePad includes both D-pad browsing and an app-wide pointer, but its main advantage is BrowsePad Connect, which lets your phone take over as a trackpad-style remote for smoother browsing.
Yes. BrowsePad supports tab creation, tab switching, tab closing, and close-all-tabs support.

items.q: Can websites request a new window?
items.a: Yes. Sites that try to open a new browser window are handled by opening that content in a new tab.

items.q: Does BrowsePad support bookmarks and history?
items.a: Yes. Bookmarks and history each have dedicated internal pages, and BrowsePad also restores previously open non-internal tabs after relaunch.

items.q: Can I switch sites into desktop mode?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad supports per-site switching between Adaptive and Desktop view modes, plus a default view mode setting.
Yes. BrowsePad confirms downloads before starting them and uses Android DownloadManager integration where supported.

items.q: What if a page fails or has SSL issues?
items.a: BrowsePad blocks insecure certificate flows and shows a friendly internal error page for common failures like timeouts, SSL problems, redirect loops, and host lookup errors.
BrowsePad Connect is built into the browser as an internal page, including a native TV Connect tab with QR pairing and controller status details.

cta.title: Redefine How You Browse on TV
cta.text: Download BrowsePad for Android TV and experience web browsing built around a phone-powered trackpad, precise pointer control, and BrowsePad Connect.

brand.name: BrowsePad
brand.about: An Android TV browser focused on redefining TV web browsing through BrowsePad Connect, phone-powered trackpad input, and precise pointer control.

softwareApplication.name: BrowsePad
softwareApplication.description: BrowsePad is an Android TV browser designed to redefine TV web browsing with BrowsePad Connect, phone-powered trackpad input, precise pointer control, tabs, bookmarks, and desktop view support.
